mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as market participants price in stronger economic activity.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and strong consumer spending, boosting equity valuations over a 60-day horizon as economic confidence improves.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are negative or flat (within +/-0.5%) in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.5% over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is low while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is stable or rising moderately, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy in a growing economy, which supports higher equity valuations due to lower discount rates and healthy earnings, driving positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with accommodative policy
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances, or if the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant in predicting returns (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, which translates quickly into positive market sentiment and short-term S&P 500 gains over a 5-day horizon, especially when overall economic growth is strong and demand is healthy.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns do not show a positive average in periods where INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% and GDPC1 1-year growth is above 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals a recovery in the housing market and broader economic confidence, leading to increased construction activity and consumer spending on durable goods, which boosts corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where HOUST shows a 10% 3-month average increase during identified recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, leading to increased consumer income and spending, which directly supports corporate profitability and S&P 500 valuations, driving positive 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: any regime with positive employment growth
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A scenario of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) coupled with sluggish GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates stagflationary pressures, which erodes corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as investors anticipate slower earnings and potential policy tightening.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of triggered instances, or if the interaction term between high inflation and low GDP growth does not significantly predict negative returns (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) provides a fundamental floor for equity valuations, and during periods of heightened market uncertainty, positive GDP reports can quickly alleviate fears and drive short-term S&P 500 rallies over a 5-day horizon as investors re-rate future earnings potential.
regime_hint: periods of high market volatility or uncertainty
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns are negative or flat (within +/-0.2%) in more than 40% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% during periods of VIX above 25.
